steps.title: Decode the function notation
steps.content:
The problem states that $x$ represents years after the investment and $f(x)$ gives the value in dollars.

When we write $f(2)$, we substitute $x = 2$, which means 2 years after the investment was made.

The statement tells us that $f(2) \approx 9,474.08$, so the value is approximately 9,474.08 dollars.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice against this interpretation
steps.content:
• A: Mentions 2% interest, but the function specifies 3% — incorrect
  • B: Uses 9,474.08 days (not years) and claims the value is approximately 2 times greater than the initial investment — incorrect
  • D: Describes 9,474.08 as the increase from the initial amount, implying a total of $9,000 + 9,474.08$ — incorrect
  • C: States 2 years after the investment was made, the value of the CD was approximately 9,474.08 dollars — this is exactly what $f(2) \approx 9,474.08$ means
That matches choice C — C is correct.
